raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$1400.00} by a jewelry merchant operating inside XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX. The charge was for two necklaces purchased during my trip. 

I returned both necklaces for the following reasons : One of the necklaces caused an immediate and painful skin rash upon wearing ( documented in photos ). 
The quality of the other necklace did not match what was promised during the sale and appeared to be of inferior construction and value. 

I shipped both items back via XXXX XXXX and included a signed, written statement formally relinquishing ownership and requesting a full refund. I chose to use XXXX  instead of returning the items in person because the merchant had become verbally aggressive and intimidating, and I did not feel safe returning alone. XXXX provided a fully traceable method that protected both parties and ensured proper documentation. 

Despite proper delivery procedures, the package was refused by the merchant and remains unclaimed in XXXX. I submitted : XXXX  shipment proof and tracking My signed return statement Photos of the allergic skin reaction CitiBank denied the dispute on XX/XX/year>, stating the timeframe to resolve has passed. However, this denial contradicts the actual timeline : Citi reopened the case on XX/XX/year> The merchants response is dated XX/XX/year> XXXX day after the denial letter Citis dispute portal still lists the case as under review I followed all dispute procedures in good faith and within all deadlines. I returned the jewelry with full documentation. The charge should never have been finalized against me when the return was refused through no fault of my own.
